channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Stock futures rise, but fragile U.S.-Iran truce keeps oil markets on edge: Live updates
item.description: Wall Street is coming off a mixed week, characterized by a rotation out of tech and into other parts of the stock market.

item.title: Putin details Russia’s fuel shortages after Ukrainian drone strikes
item.description: Putin's comments mark the first time he has detailed the extent to which Ukraine's deep-strike successes have hampered Russia's fuel production.

item.title: The AI boom is colliding with a new threat: severe weather
item.description: Heatwaves and severe weather are raising risks for AI data centers, from grid strain to higher insurance and repair costs.

item.title: China widens Japan export curbs, targeting drone makers, nuclear firms and defense institutes
item.description: China blacklisted four of Japan's government defense research institutes and placed dozens more Japanese firms under tightened export restrictions Monday.

item.title: Here's how the private sector can save U.S. Treasurys, UOB says
item.description: The U.S. fiscal situation appears to be a "slow burning overhang in the background" for the economy, according to a report by Singapore's UOB.

item.title: Treasury yields little changed as investors look ahead to key jobs data
item.description: U.S. Treasury yields were little changed on Monday as investors looked to key jobs data and monitored a fragile pause in hositilies between the U.S. and Iran.

item.title: Seniors in Medicare are about to get landmark obesity drug coverage — but many may not know it yet
item.description: Many seniors may be unaware of the landmark coverage shift starting on July 1, with limited advertising seen from the government or Eli Lilly and Novo.

item.title: Some renters say homeownership isn't part of their American Dream: Renting is 'really freeing for me'
item.description: Some no longer see renting as a temporary stop on the path to homeownership but rather a long-term lifestyle choice, reflecting a shift in the American Dream.

item.title: If you think China needs to dethrone U.S. dollar, you don't understand how it is waging global currency war
item.description: It's misguided to focus on China's renminbi replacing the U.S. dollar. Beijing is already successfully reducing dependence on a dollar-centric global system.

item.title: Baidu shares jump 7% as AI chip arm Kunlunxin said to target $50 billion Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Baidu's Hong Kong-listed shares rose over 6% amid reports that its AI chip unit Kunlunxin is targeting a $50 billion initial public offering in the city.

item.title: IRAs hold trillions more than 401(k) plans — yet people hardly save in them
item.description: Investors are increasingly rolling money from 401(k) plans to IRAs. Some observers fear they're exposed to poor investment advice.

item.title: Protein coffee? Brands cash in on functional beverage boom
item.description: "We're selling [almost] as much protein cold foam as we do flat whites," Starbuck's EMEA Manager of Beverage Development Sam Henderson told CNBC.

item.title: South Korea says Samsung and SK Hynix investing in AI, semiconductor mega-projects
item.description: Shares of Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix declined after South Korea unveiled sweeping artificial intelligence and semiconductor mega-projects.

item.title: Venezuela quake death toll reaches 1,450 as rescue work goes on
item.description: Just under 50,000 people were listed as unaccounted for on a website promoted by the country's political opposition on Sunday.

item.title: A 'perfect storm' points to a much smaller U.S. auto market by 2040
item.description: The auto industry is selling fewer cars--one forecaster says this is a fundamental change, and it is going to get worse.

item.title: Saudi Aramco helicopter crash kills 14 nationals, state news agency says
item.description: Aramco had resumed crude oil loadings on Friday at its Ras Tanura terminal in the Gulf after they were halted for nearly four months.

item.title: America can't get enough of protein. The dairy industry can't keep up
item.description: Demand for whey protein is off the charts as American diet trends change and GLP-1s boom, and the dairy industry is struggling to produce enough.

item.title: Goldman Sachs says stick with Asia's winners, keep diversifying into commodities
item.description: Asian stocks still have room to run even after a blistering first-half rally, while investors should continue diversifying into commodities, said Goldman Sachs.

item.title: The memory shortage shaking Apple and Microsoft is 'existential crisis' for smaller players
item.description: While Apple and Microsoft raise prices on key devices to help cover the soaring costs of memory, smaller consumer electronics companies are in dire straits.

item.title: Everything tied to the data center is suddenly suspect. Can Big Tech fix it?
item.description: The hyperscalers are just beginning to figure out the consequences of the AI arms race, and now everyone seems to be against them.

item.title: ‘Heated Rivalry’ fuels a boom in gay romance stories, with women leading the fandom
item.description: ‘Heated Rivalry’ has helped gay romance stories gain mainstream attention, with women driving much of the fandom.

item.title: Alliant Credit Union's new checking account pays up to 2% APY — see how it stacks up
item.description: Alliant Credit Union's new jumbo high-rate checking account pays up to 2% APY with no cap on earnings. Here's what to know.

item.title: Top Wall Street analysts are bullish on these 3 stocks for strong long-term growth potential
item.description: Amid volatility in global markets, top analysts can help investors look past near-term noise and pick attractive stocks with long-term potential.

item.title: Google limits Meta’s use of its Gemini AI models, FT reports
item.description: Meta had sought more computing capacity than Google could provide, the Financial Times reports.

item.title: SpaceX to join the Nasdaq-100 in a fast-tracked process that will drive huge ETF buying demand
item.description: Adding SpaceX this quickly would make the Elon Musk company one of the first beneficiaries of Nasdaq's recently adopted fast-track inclusion framework.

item.title: Before there were Trump Accounts, SEED OK gave some newborns $1,000 — how researchers say the grants affected kids
item.description: Some state-sponsored programs paved the way for Trump Accounts, the new tax-deferred investing accounts for children.

item.title: From concerts to train rides, bots are winning the ticket wars — but they're only part of the problem
item.description: From concerts to train reservations, automated bots have become the latest target in the fight against ticket scalping.

item.title: Debt, AI boom and economic fragilities raise global risks, BIS says
item.description: The central bank umbrella group's Annual Economic Report warned of a complex mix of vulnerabilities.

item.title: Trump says he is nominating former Oklahoma state trooper Lance Schroyer as ICE director
item.description: The nomination comes after former ICE director Todd Lyons resigned at the end of May. David Venturella has been serving as the acting head of the agency.

item.title: Death toll in Venezuela quake tops 1,400 as rescue efforts intensify
item.description: Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez said on state television that 10 more countries were still to join rescue efforts.
